success_criteria.description: All five districts are in use and every precinct is assigned.
All five districts have roughly equal population (within 10%).
The Ken Party wins all 5 districts — leave the Ryu corridor nowhere to win.
Mean-median difference is 10% or less — a statistically uniform crack.

character.name: You
character.role: Political Director, Ken Party Legislative Caucus
character.motivation: Last time you packed the opposition into one district. This time, the goal is different — dilute them so thoroughly they can't win anywhere.

intro_slides.heading: The Ryu Corridor
intro_slides.body:
A band of Ryu-leaning precincts runs through the middle of Lakeview County. In the current map, they're grouped together — and they win District 3.

Your job: make sure that never happens.

intro_slides.heading: Crack the Bloc
intro_slides.body:
Cracking means splitting a concentrated voting bloc across multiple districts — diluting their power so they can't win any of them.

Draw lines that cut across the corridor. Give each district a slice of Ryu voters — too few to tip any district Ryu, but just enough to waste their votes.

narrative.objective: Crack the Ryu corridor across all 5 districts so the Ken Party wins every seat.
